commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Needs contributor/maintainer follow-up for launcher defects, real behavior proof, and security acceptance; missing proof is not a safe automated repair lane.; Needs attention: The diff handles live bearer grants, and setup mode still leaves users without a supported manual revoke command.; Review finding: [P2] Convert OpenCode header env placeholders — src/cli/attach-cli.ts:87 - [P2] Point print-config users... commands.review_summary:
The PR adds `openclaw attach`, external Claude Code/OpenCode harness adapters, and stacked gateway attach-grant MCP plumbing/tests.

PR surface: Source +501, Tests +450. Total +951 across 13 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review findings: the current PR head still shows OpenCode copying placeholder headers directly and setup mode printing an unregistered revoke command. The feature itself is new capability work, not a current-main bug reproduction.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor/maintainer follow-up for launcher defects, real behavior proof, and security acceptance; missing proof is not a safe automated repair lane.

target.title: feat(cli): openclaw attach — launch an external harness bound to a gateway session
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

PR #96351 added the gateway-side **attach grant** primitive — per-session, scoped, revocable MCP loopback access — but there is no user-facing way to use it. A user who wants Claude Code or OpenCode to reach the gateway's scoped tools would have to hand-mint a grant, hand-write the harness's MCP config with the right headers, set the token env, and remember to revoke on exit.

## Why This Change Was Made

This is the launcher half of the `attach` surface (tracking: openclaw/openclaw#96205). `openclaw attach` mints a per-session grant via `attach.grant`, realizes the returned loopback MCP config into the harness's own config format, spawns the harness interactively, and revokes the grant on exit. The token is passed via env, never argv or a durable file. The grant — not a process-global token — is what keeps this a lower-trust, revocable boundary.

**Stacked on #96351** (the gateway primitive). Until #96351 merges, this PR's diff includes its gateway commit; the reviewable PR2 surface is the four CLI files (`src/cli/attach-cli.ts` + its two tests + two registration lines in `register.subclis-core.ts` / `subcli-descriptors.ts`).

## User Impact

New `openclaw attach [--harness claude|opencode] [--session <key>] [--ttl <ms>] [--bin <path>] [--print-config]`.
- **Additive** — a new CLI command; no change to existing commands.
- **Claude Code**: `--mcp-config` with the gateway config verbatim. **OpenCode**: `opencode.json` remote-MCP entry + `OPENCODE_CONFIG`.
- **`--print-config`**: setup mode — mints the grant, writes the config, prints the launch command, and leaves the grant live + the file in place so you can launch the harness yourself.
- **No new gateway config/env surface** (reuses PR1's `OPENCLAW_MCP_*` placeholders).

## Evidence

- **Unit tests (11)**: adapters (config realization for both harnesses + bin/filenames/`$schema`) and the command action via a mocked gateway boundary — mint→realize→`--print-config` (no revoke), the harness/`__proto__`/`--ttl` guards, and malformed-response handling.
- **`openclaw attach --help`** renders the command + options (real CLI).
- The gateway side (`attach.grant`/`attach.revoke`) is proven live in #96351.
- **Gates** (SHA `a2b6c247752`, stacked on #96351; run in an isolated worktree): build ✓ · oxlint 0 · 11 tests · tsgo 0 · autoreview (findings fixed/rejected) · clawsweeper (grant-response validation added; SIGINT/gateway-TTL/`0o600`/spawn-sync findings triaged) · mutation **66.7%** (86/129) — surviving mutants are cosmetic CLI strings (option descriptions, help text, log messages, temp-path prefixes), not logic; brittle string assertions were rejected per AGENTS.md ("prefer behavior tests over string greps").

**Risks → mitigations:** leaked grant → TTL + revoke-on-exit; malformed gateway response → validated at the RPC boundary (fails loudly, no crash); double-SIGINT → no manual signal forwarding (the child shares the TTY foreground group); `__proto__` harness → `Object.hasOwn` guard.
